events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the required JSON directly to `/app/answer.json`. This is a code/compliance judgment task, so I’m treating the absent bonding jumper across tray expansion joints as a fail at rough-complete.

events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 90,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.92,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Cable tray expansion joints are missing bonding continuity jumpers or equivalent bonding means.\",
    \"Metal cable tray is not electrically continuous across the expansion joints, defeating the equipment grounding path required for tray systems.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop acceptance of the affected cable tray run until bonding is corrected.\",
    \"Install listed bonding jumpers or engineered bonding connections across each expansion joint per drawings, specifications, and NEC Article 392 requirements.\",
    \"Verify continuity across each expansion joint before closeout or energization.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Visible absence of bonding across expansion joints is a zero-count condition for required bonding continuity. At rough-complete the tray is installed, but the expansion joints interrupt electrical continuity, so the installation is out of tolerance and non-compliant until bonding jumpers or equivalent listed bonding means are installed and verified.\",
  \"references\": [\"NEC Article 392 - Cable Trays\", \"NEC 392.60 - Grounding and Bonding\"]
}' > /app/answer.json && jq . /app/answer.json"
→ /app
